titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 721

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=954 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:41:10.654657+00 | translation moa=955 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:41:10.645198+00

p.cl: Ranulfus de Bonkil Omnibus videntibus uel audientibus has literas Salutem .

Sciatis me
intuitu karitatis et ob amorem et reuerenciam beati Cuthberti gloriosi confessoris et pro animabus
patris mei et matris mee et pro salute anime mee et uxoris mee et omnium parentum meorum
Totum clamum quod habui et ius si quod habui uel habere potui in boscis et moris prioris et monachorum de Coldingham . Scilicet Brockesholes et Harewde et Denewde omnino eisdem
monachis sine aliquo renunciamento quietum clamasse . Et insuper sciatis quod ego et heredes mei
diuisas factas et perambulatas inter Coldingham et Bonkil Scilicet a Middeldeneshe
ued per Mereburnesheued versus occidentem usque ad Cratoketrestrete et inde per eandem
stratam usque ad Eiford predictis monachis in perpetuum pacifice tenebimus et conseruabimus .

Hiis Testibus . Patricio filio Comitis . Patricio filio Ade . Roberto de Vpsetelingtona .
Dauid Marescallo Thoma de Gordona . Matheo de Normanuilla . Iohanne de
sancte michaele . Willelmo de Veteri ponte . Stephano de La Warderobe . Henrico de prender
gest . Willelmo de Witeslade Alano de Swintona . Patricio de Prestona . Symone filio
Thome . Waltero de Paxtona . Andrea de paxtona . Willelmo de Lumesdene . et multis aliis ;
Ranulf of Bunkle to all who view or hear these letters sends greeting.

Know me, moved by charitable love, out of love and reverence for blessed Cuthbert the glorious confessor, and for the souls of my father and my mother and for the salvation of my own soul and the soul of my wife and all my ancestors, to have quitclaimed the entire claim which I had, and the right which I had or was able to have, in the woods and moors of the prior and monks of Coldingham, that is, Brockesholes, Harewood, Denewood, entirely to the same monks, without any renouncing. In addition, know that I and my heirs shall maintain and preserve to the aforesaid monks in perpetual peace the boundaries made and perambulated between Coldingham and Bunkle, that is, from 'Middeldenesheued' by 'Mereburnesheued' westwards to 'Cratoketrestrete' and from there by the same path to Eiford.

With these witnesses, Patrick son of the earl, Patrick son of Adam, Robert of Upsettlington, David the Marischal, Thomas of Gordon, Matthew of Normanville, John of St-Michel, William de Vieuxpont, Stephen of the Wardrobe, Henry of Prenderguest, William of Wideslade, Alan of Swinton, Patrick of Preston, Simon son of Thomas, Walter of Paxton, Andrew of Paxton, William of Lumsdaine, and many others.
